fresh_facts.claim: US President Donald Trump announced a military blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz on April 13, 2026, deploying at least 13 US warships to halt tankers and cut off Iran's energy exports following failed nuclear talks in Pakistan.
At least 13 US warships are enforcing a blockade targeting ships entering or exiting Iranian ports to pressure Iran economically and diplomatically amid a fragile ceasefire.

Iranian state media released footage on April 12, 2026, showing IRGC forces issuing a 'last warning' to US destroyers USS Michael Murphy and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. in the Strait of Hormuz, claiming missiles and drones were locked on them.
US CENTCOM denied Iranian claims of a standoff or US retreat involving USS Michael Murphy and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. in the Strait of Hormuz.
Additional US forces, including underwater drones, were set to join mine-clearing efforts in the Strait of Hormuz where Iran has laid mines since the war began on February 28, 2026.
Trump claimed Iran's navy, air force, anti-aircraft systems, radar, and leadership are neutralized with no Iranian business occurring in the strait.
US-Iran nuclear talks in Islamabad ended around April 12-13, 2026, after over 20 hours of failed negotiations centered on Iran's nuclear program.

Israeli airstrikes on June 15, 2026, targeted Iranian regime institutions including the Justice Ministry, Foreign Affairs Ministry, Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS), and Law Enforcement Command (LEC) headquarters in Tehran.

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the killing of IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Brigadier General Mohammad Kazemi, his deputy Hassan Mohaghegh, and officer Mohsen Bagheri in the June 15, 2026 airstrikes.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei appointed Major General Amir Hatami as overall Artesh commander, replacing Abdol Rahim Mousavi, signaling internal military adjustments under pressure.
IDF attacks on energy infrastructure occurred on June 14, 2026, followed by an airstrike on an airbase near Mashhad.
An Iranian Armed Forces General Staff outlet called for Basij paramilitary deployment for widespread patrols, stop-and-search posts, and countering alleged Mossad infiltration to suppress dissent.
Iran conducted two ballistic missile waves against Israel since June 14, 2026, but its Axis of Resistance partners have not significantly intervened.
Iranian officials stated no return to US-Iran nuclear negotiations will occur until Israel halts attacks and Iran completes retaliation.
A senior Lebanese official pushed for a temporary ceasefire, with Israel-Lebanon talks set to start the following week per Axios reporting.
Iran maintained internet blackout and repression tools despite arrests as of April 2026, following a fragile US-Iran ceasefire.
CSIS analysis on April 8, 2026, highlighted unresolved Iranian nuclear ambitions and shadow war risks.
Institute for the Study of War described prior protests as the most serious unrest ever faced by the Iranian regime.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces compounding pressures from a sustained US naval blockade (initiated April 13), Israeli airstrikes decimating regime security leadership (June 14-15), and intensified internal repression with over 700 arrests and mass executions by early July, while diplomatic avenues remain closed and succession uncertainty continues amid military command restructuring.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 15, 2026, killed IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Mohammad Kazemi and other senior security officials, targeting key regime institutions (Justice, Foreign Affairs, MOIS, LEC) while Supreme Leader Khamenei replaced the Artesh commander, signaling severe institutional disruption and succession pressures.
